title: jsdelivr.net CDN poisoning for instantsearch.js@4.75.6
body:
### 🐛 Current behavior

Hi, I’m a software developer and security researcher and I think the jsdelivr CDN link in [Algolia’s documentation](url) for instantsearch.js@4.75.6 was hacked/poisoned. I have been working on a laptop disconnected from the internet with instantsearch.js@4.75.6 that I downloaded from that link on December 16th (copy/paste, then transferred), and my page would consistently, randomly, redirect to “[https://progressstream.com/click.php?key=ll42mxrpq6zsg1hn1bvj&PRICE=0.05&SITE=4209&id=0](https://progressstream.com/click.php?key=ll42mxrpq6zsg1hn1bvj&PRICE=0.05&SITE=4209&id=0)”. I had Firefox’s network monitor up at the time and noticed many 404s for several Facebook API redirects too, including a few to “https://facebook.com/hk.observatory”.

This is what I downloaded on December 16th: https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/instantsearch.js@4.75.6/dist/instantsearch.production.min.js

I know you released 4.75.7 very recently so I moved to that but as of yesterday, Algolia’s [docs installation page](https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/building-search-ui/installation/js/#directly-in-your-page) still had the link to 4.75.6 listed from jsdelivr.

I have instead been using the unpkg CDN version of 4.75.7 this week without issue, however I did not find that CDN documented anywhere: https://unpkg.com/instantsearch.js@4.75.7/dist/instantsearch.production.min.js

### 🔍 Steps to reproduce

Being on a disconnected laptop meant the page tried to refresh but went nowhere, leaving me confused, especially when it happened a few times in the past couple weeks. I’m not using any other libraries and I’m writing a pure JavaScript/CSS/HTML page from scratch to talk to Elasticsearch. When it occurred, Firefox's network monitor had a link that pointed at the instantsearch.js code as the originator, but since it is minified I couldn't isolate the section that spawned the redirect. I tried manually parsing the code to look for uuencoded/base64 encoded strings that could contain the hijack code, but didn't see anything at first glance.

### Live reproduction

https://codesandbox.io/s/github/algolia/instantsearch/tree/templates/instantsearch.js

### 💭 Expected behavior

I expected the instantsearch.js library to function without redirecting me at random times to arbitrary websites.

### Package version

instantsearch.js@4.75.6

### Operating system

CentOS 7.9

### Browser

Firefox 115.11.0esr

### Code of Conduct

- [X] I agree to follow this project's Code of Conduct
